The '''Mordageen''' were a [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]] [[Species/Legends|species]] from the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Mordagon]]. The species was bipedal in form with pink skin and pointed [[Ear/Legends|ears]]. One notorious Mordageen was the [[Smuggler/Legends|smuggler]] [[Bendix Fust]], who worked for the [[Dug/Legends|Dug]] [[Crime lord/Legends|crime lord]] [[Sebolto]]. Captured by [[Republic Security Force/Legends|Republic Security Forces]], Fust revealed all he knew of his employer's organization, and was placed in [[Desolation Alley]], a [[Prison/Legends|prison]] on [[Oovo IV/Legends|Oovo IV]]. There, he was broken out by two [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunters]] so that Sebolto could get his revenge.

==Biology and appearance==
[[File:Bendix_Fust.jpg|thumb|left|150px|Bendix Fust in prison.]]
The Mordageen were a bipedal [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]] [[Species/Legends|species]] native to the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Mordagon]],<ref name="Prima" /> located in the [[Expansion Region/Legends|Expansion Region]].<ref name="Atlas">''[[The Essential Atlas]]''</ref> They had wrinkled reddish orange<ref name="CSWE II 330" /> or pink skin and [[Limb/Legends|hands]] with three fingers and an opposable thumb. Mordageen had a reasonably flat face with a stubby [[Nose/Legends|nose]], and pointy [[Ear/Legends|ears]]. A shock of hair grew from their scalp, blond in coloration,<ref name="SW:BH" /> with stubble around their chin and mouth.<ref name="State of the Arts">{{GamerCite|10|State of the Arts}}</ref> Mordageen stood 1.75 [[Meter/Legends|meters]] tall on average.<ref name="height" />

==History==
Individual Mordageen had contact with the wider [[The galaxy/Legends|galaxy]] by the time of the [[Battle of Naboo/Legends|Battle of Naboo]]<ref name="SW:BH" /> in [[32 BBY/Legends|32 BBY]].<ref name="NEC">''[[The New Essential Chronology]]''</ref> One such Mordageen was [[Bendix Fust]], a [[Smuggler/Legends|smuggler]] working for the [[Dug/Legends|Dug]] [[Crime lord/Legends|crime lord]] [[Sebolto]] in his [[Death stick/Legends|death stick]] operation. However, the Mordageen was captured by the [[Republic Security Force/Legends|Republic Security Force]] in a sting operation on a transfer station in the [[Gazzari system/Legends|Gazzari system]]. In exchange for a reduced prison sentence, the Mordageen told the Security Forces all he knew of the [[Sebolto's drug cartel]]. He was then placed in a maximum security [[Prison/Legends|prison]] on the [[Asteroid/Legends|asteroid]] [[Oovo IV/Legends|Oovo IV]],<ref name="SW:BH" /> a complex known as [[Desolation Alley]].<ref name="Star Wars: Episode I Racer">[[Star Wars: Episode I Racer|''Star Wars'': Episode I ''Racer'']]</ref> Wanting revenge, Sebolto placed a [[Bounty/Legends|bounty]] on his former employee's head. Independently of each other, two [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunters]], the [[Clawdite/Legends|Clawdite]] [[Zam Wesell/Legends|Zam Wesell]] and the [[Mandalorian/Legends|Mandalorian]] [[Jango Fett/Legends|Jango Fett]], broke into the prison to retrieve Fust. They eventually worked together to break him out, and handed him over to Sebolto on [[Malastare/Legends|Malastare]].<ref name="SW:BH" />

==Behind the scenes==
The Mordageen first appeared in ''[[Star Wars: Bounty Hunter]]'', a [[Star Wars video games|video game]] produced by [[LucasArts]] in [[2002]] and released for the [[Wikipedia:PlayStation 2|PlayStation 2]] and the [[Wikipedia:Nintendo GameCube|GameCube]] platforms. In the game, the species was not named, but was represented by Bendix Fust. He was later identified as a Mordageen in the ''[[Star Wars: Bounty Hunter: Prima's Official Strategy Guide]]'', published by [[Prima Games]] in [[November]] 2002.
